steps.title: Use the cylinder formula
steps.content:
The volume of a right circular cylinder is
$$
V=\pi r^2h
$$
Here, the radius is $13$ meters and the height is $10$ meters.

steps.title: Substitute and compute
steps.content: Substitute the given radius and height. $$ V=\pi(13)^2(10)=\pi(169)(10)=1{,}690\pi $$ The answer is D.
